replies.msg:
由於好多人都冇睇Scott Morrison (PM) 同 Immigration minister 講乜，所以我睇完之後幫大家做左個整合。我相信依家澳洲政府所講嘅Safe Heaven未來會更新，依家應該係大概嘅計劃，好多人都話冇香港手足份，據我所知，前排係有手足到步澳洲，但係佢地清況會唔同於我地其他香港人，因為可能有犯罪紀錄，所以佢地應該係用政治難民身分去apply澳洲嘅難民Visa。依家所謂Safe Heaven應該係比所有因不滿國安法而選擇離開香港嘅香港人，五眼係想接收大部分香港人，除左比港共拘捕過嘅手足，想移民嘅都唔係用難民身分，因為不符合國際難民定義。以下係我今日整合好嘅資料(英文)，睇唔明請留言。
Summary of Australia Safe Heaven (09/07/2020 updated)
1. Student visa (Current & Future HK students)
• Current & Future HK students who successfully conclude of your study (successful graduation)  Eligible to applying a 5-year Temporary Graduate visa  Pathway to PR (permanent residency)
2. Currently Holding Temporary Graduate visa (Subclass 485)
• The visa will be extended to be 5-year long from now on
• Automatically or manually extended? Unknown

3. Student visa/studying in regional campus (Current & Future HK students)
• Continue with current regime  can get PR after 3 years (express pathways to PR)

4. Temporary Skilled visa Holders [currently holding] (from HK and Currently in AUS atm)
• Estimated to be around 600 ppl now in AUS
• Eligible for additional 5 years to stay in AUS  pathway to PR

5. Temporary skilled visa Holders [Future] (from HK)
• Eligible for additional 5 years to stay in AUS  pathway to PR
• Provided that they meet the existing criteria: fit with one of the skilled shortage criteria
• LIST will be updated shortly
• LIST will be a significantly reduced LIST  less job types for applicants

Labor market testing
• Labor market testing from the sponsoring employers to prove that they are unable to find an Australian to do the job
• Then, the employers can sponsor the employees to get PR and thereafter citizenship

6. Global Talent Temporary visa Scheme
• *IT field (most wanted talents) but not limited to IT field only
• Come to AUS for work on a temporary basis as if employers particular will pay above the high-income threshold
• Pathway to PR after 5 years
• Prioritizing HK applicants for this scheme
• Provide additional resources to target particular individuals:
• Entrepreneurs  multiplying the labor market (create jobs for AUS)
• Tech-talented

7. Business Investment Program
• Prioritizing applicants from HK for this scheme



For Businesses with Regional Headquarters presently in HK
• AUS Gov is currently developing new economic incentives for export-oriented HK-based business to relocate to AUS
• VISA pathways for all critical staff to come to AUS  Pathways to residency
• The immigration minister claimed there are over 1000 international businesses (Financial, consultant businesses etc.) are looking to relocate elsewhere and he wished them to come to AUS

FYI
Pathway to PR (not provided yet)
• Either remain the same or adjustment for HKers

Temporary Graduate visa (Subclass 485)
• The Originally stay is around 2 years
• Now it is extended to be 5 years
• Automatically or manually extended? Unknown
謝澳洲巴
幫推
但澳洲要去狗屋拎良民證，英國暫時仲係首選
Regional current regime 係咪即係491

https://www.pm.gov.au/media/hong-kong

Students
Current and future students from Hong Kong will be eligible for a five year temporary graduate visa on the successful conclusion of their studies, with a pathway to permanent residency after five years.
Former students already on a temporary graduate visa will be eligible for an extension of five years from today in addition to the time they’ve already been in Australia, with a pathway to permanent residency at the end of that period.
Students who study at a regional campus will continue to be able to access a pathway to permanent residency after three years.
Temporary skilled visas
Current temporary skilled visa holders from Hong Kong will be eligible for an extension of five years from today in addition to the time they’ve already been in Australia, with a pathway to permanent residency at the end of that period.
Future Hong Kong applicants for temporary skilled visas will be eligible for a five year visa, provided they:
meet occupational skills lists and Labour Market Testing requirements; or
qualify through the Global Talent temporary visa scheme, which is for exceptional talent where the sponsoring employer pays above the Fair Work High Income Threshold of $153,600.
These future temporary skilled visa holders will also have a pathway to permanent residency after five years.
There are almost 10,000 existing temporary skilled, temporary graduate and student visa holders in Australia who will be eligible for these special arrangements, with a further 2,500 outside Australia and 1,250 applications on hand.
Attracting the best and brightest
The Government will bolster efforts to attract Hong Kong’s best and brightest through the Global Talent and Business Innovation and Investment Programs, both part of the permanent migration program.
These programs will be prioritised and a dedicated Global Talent officer will focus on facilitating the Hong Kong caseload.
To support future applications we will re-open our visa application centre in Hong Kong which shut down during COVID-19.
Attracting businesses
New incentives will be developed to attract export-oriented Hong Kong based businesses to relocate to Australia. As well as economic incentives, there will be permanent visa pathways available for all critical Hong Kong based staff of the relocated business. The government will particularly target businesses that presently operate their regional headquarters out of Hong Kong who may be looking to relocate to a democratic country.

https://www.pm.gov.au/media/hong-kong

Students
Current and future students from Hong Kong will be eligible for a five year temporary graduate visa on the successful conclusion of their studies, with a pathway to permanent residency after five years.

Former students already on a temporary graduate visa will be eligible for an extension of five years from today in addition to the time they’ve already been in Australia, with a pathway to permanent residency at the end of that period.

Students who study at a regional campus will continue to be able to access a pathway to permanent residency after three years.

Temporary skilled visas
Current temporary skilled visa holders from Hong Kong will be eligible for an extension of five years from today in addition to the time they’ve already been in Australia, with a pathway to permanent residency at the end of that period.

Future Hong Kong applicants for temporary skilled visas will be eligible for a five year visa, provided they:

meet occupational skills lists and Labour Market Testing requirements; or
qualify through the Global Talent temporary visa scheme, which is for exceptional talent where the sponsoring employer pays above the Fair Work High Income Threshold of $153,600.

These future temporary skilled visa holders will also have a pathway to permanent residency after five years.

There are almost 10,000 existing temporary skilled, temporary graduate and student visa holders in Australia who will be eligible for these special arrangements, with a further 2,500 outside Australia and 1,250 applications on hand.

Attracting the best and brightest
The Government will bolster efforts to attract Hong Kong’s best and brightest through the Global Talent and Business Innovation and Investment Programs, both part of the permanent migration program.

These programs will be prioritised and a dedicated Global Talent officer will focus on facilitating the Hong Kong caseload.

To support future applications we will re-open our visa application centre in Hong Kong which shut down during COVID-19.

Attracting businesses
New incentives will be developed to attract export-oriented Hong Kong based businesses to relocate to Australia. As well as economic incentives, there will be permanent visa pathways available for all critical Hong Kong based staff of the relocated business.

The government will particularly target businesses that presently operate their regional headquarters out of Hong Kong who may be looking to relocate to a democratic country.

pinned.msg:
https://www.pm.gov.au/media/hong-kong

Students
Current and future students from Hong Kong will be eligible for a five year temporary graduate visa on the successful conclusion of their studies, with a pathway to permanent residency after five years.

Former students already on a temporary graduate visa will be eligible for an extension of five years from today in addition to the time they’ve already been in Australia, with a pathway to permanent residency at the end of that period.

Students who study at a regional campus will continue to be able to access a pathway to permanent residency after three years.

Temporary skilled visas
Current temporary skilled visa holders from Hong Kong will be eligible for an extension of five years from today in addition to the time they’ve already been in Australia, with a pathway to permanent residency at the end of that period.

Future Hong Kong applicants for temporary skilled visas will be eligible for a five year visa, provided they:

meet occupational skills lists and Labour Market Testing requirements; or
qualify through the Global Talent temporary visa scheme, which is for exceptional talent where the sponsoring employer pays above the Fair Work High Income Threshold of $153,600.

These future temporary skilled visa holders will also have a pathway to permanent residency after five years.

There are almost 10,000 existing temporary skilled, temporary graduate and student visa holders in Australia who will be eligible for these special arrangements, with a further 2,500 outside Australia and 1,250 applications on hand.

Attracting the best and brightest
The Government will bolster efforts to attract Hong Kong’s best and brightest through the Global Talent and Business Innovation and Investment Programs, both part of the permanent migration program.

These programs will be prioritised and a dedicated Global Talent officer will focus on facilitating the Hong Kong caseload.

To support future applications we will re-open our visa application centre in Hong Kong which shut down during COVID-19.

Attracting businesses
New incentives will be developed to attract export-oriented Hong Kong based businesses to relocate to Australia. As well as economic incentives, there will be permanent visa pathways available for all critical Hong Kong based staff of the relocated business.

The government will particularly target businesses that presently operate their regional headquarters out of Hong Kong who may be looking to relocate to a democratic country.
